codeReview.summary: Re-review after fix attempt. The blocking issues from the prior review are STILL PERSISTING. The ExtractionConfig::default() still sets store_path to '.skills' instead of '.swell/skills/'. The TrajectoryAnalyzer still has #[allow(dead_code)] attribute and its store field is never used for any database operations.

issues.description: STILL PERSISTS: ExtractionConfig::default() sets store_path to '.skills' but AGENTS.md specifies user-extensible skills should be in '.swell/skills/'. The feature description states 'Stores reusable procedures in .skills directory' but AGENTS.md section 'Project Structure' shows '.swell/' as the configuration directory with 'skills/' as a subdirectory. The default should be '.swell/skills' to align with project conventions.

STILL PERSISTS: TrajectoryAnalyzer has #[allow(dead_code)] attribute. The store field is defined at line 142 but never used - all analyzer methods (analyze, extract_file_patterns, extract_tool_patterns, extract_test_patterns, extract_validation_patterns, deduplicate_patterns) only operate on the in-memory TrajectoryData without any database queries. The #[allow(dead_code)] is unjustified and should either be removed (if the store will be used) or the field should be removed entirely.

summary: This is a re-run review. The 2 prior blocking issues are STILL PERSISTING with no code changes: (1) ExtractionConfig::default() store_path remains '.skills' instead of '.swell/skills/', (2) TrajectoryAnalyzer still has #[allow(dead_code)] and unused store field. The code at commit 723e11b is unchanged.
